introBody: Alright, paperwork. We are calling this thing Space Cowboys, Inc. You and me, partners, fifty-fifty. The HQ goes on Mars — cheap dust, decent gravity, nobody breathing down our necks.
Before we drop a pin on the rust planet we need a little street cred. People only talk to operators who have logged hours. So here is the run, in order:
1) Knock out an EVA contract — proves you can suit up and clock out clean.
2) Pick up two asteroid jobs off my board, so the lander log reads Mercer twice.
3) Bolt on Cryo Insulation Lvl 1 — Mars gets cold the second the sun drops.
4) Then come visit me at Mars. We open the office.
5) Sign the welcome paperwork by running an orbital out of Mars — first delivery as a real Cowboys office.
Accept and I will start ringing your inbox with the next move every time you finish one.

steps.subject: Suit Up — One EVA
flavor: Hey, you got Jay.
First one's the suit job. Pick any EVA contract off the board, get out there, finish it without losing a glove or a tether. Easy money for a guy with hands.
Close it out clean and the dispatch ledger ticks over. One down.
— Jay

steps.subject: Two Rocks With My Name On Them
flavor: Hey, you got Jay.
Now we put a Mercer stamp on the lander logs. Take two asteroid contracts off my list specifically — not the consortium boards, mine — boots on the rock both times.
I am the dispatcher and the pilot and the guy who buys you a beer when it's done. Welcome to early-stage equity. Two of mine and we read like a real outfit on paper.
— Jay

steps.subject: Bolt On Cryo Insulation
flavor: Hey, you got Jay.
Mars is not Venus, but the long nights bite. Get Cryo Insulation Lvl 1 fitted at any spaceport engineering bay before you head out.
Trust me. The first time the cabin frost crawls up the inside of the canopy you will wish you had paid the credits.
— Jay

steps.subject: Come To Mars
flavor: Hey, you got Jay.
Burn for Mars. Park in orbit, dock at the spaceport, walk into the engineering bay — I'll be the guy holding two coffees and a key card.
Welcome to HQ, partner. The kiosk is yours. Anytime you wanna come home, you point the nose at Mars and we will leave the lights on.
— Jay

steps.subject: First Job From The Mars Desk
flavor: Hey, you got Jay.

Welcome aboard, partner. First order of business — pick up an orbital contract from our own kiosk here at Mars and run it. Doesn't matter where the cargo is parked; what matters is the slip says Space Cowboys, Inc. on the giver line.
Walk into the Mars spaceport mission board, grab the planetary shuttle job on offer, fly the orbital pickup, and ring me when the cargo is in the hold. That is the official ribbon-cutting.
— Jay

completionBody: We're official. Space Cowboys, Inc. has a permanent kiosk in the Mars spaceport. Your name is on the door, sort of — I drew it in marker, it'll outlast the hull plating.
From now on you can fast-travel home to Mars from the star map any time you want. Click the planet, confirm, and the orbital traffic controllers will pretend they slotted you in legitimately.
Drinks at the kiosk. On me.
